headline: Penang Transport Master Plan Faces RM28 Billion Gap
context: Penang transport master plan including LRT highway and undersea tunnel faces RM28 billion funding gap after federal cuts.

cards.big: Penang transport plan faces RM28 billion shortfall after federal cuts
cards.sub: LRT tunnel and highway package may be scaled back without additional funding

cards.big: Penang LRT requires RM9.6 billion for 29km connecting airport to George Town
cards.sub: Federal government offered RM2 billion in matching versus RM5 billion requested

cards.big: Undersea tunnel connecting island to mainland estimated at RM6.3 billion
cards.sub: Project studied since 2013 but construction has not commenced

cards.big: Penang traffic congestion costs RM4.2 billion annually in lost productivity
cards.sub: Average commuter spends 85 minutes daily in traffic versus 65 minutes in KL

cards.big: Funding gaps test whether state transport ambition can proceed without federal support
RM28 billion funding gap for Penang transport — ambition without fiscal backing produces plans, not systems.
